titleStmt.title: Letter from John Bay to Sir Matthew Nathan, 18 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John Bay

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter addressed to Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939). The letter writer states that the volunteers of the Belfast Volunteer Defence Corps, under John Bristow, have proved useful on patrol. The writer believes they would be more useful if they were sworn in as special constables and understands that such an application has been made he wishes to make his support for the idea known.Sir Matthew Nathan was the under-secretary for Ireland from 1914 to 1916. In 1916 Nathan offered his resignation following the outbreak of the Easter Rising.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/7964/10

correspAction.placeName: Headquarters, Belfast Garrison, Victoria Barracks, Belfast, County Antrim, Ireland.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
HEADQUARTERS, BELFAST GARRISON, Victoria Barracks, BELFAST. 18.4.16 My dear Nathan, The Volunteers here under
Mr. John Bristow, Commandant, are
affiliated properly and organised â
some 220 or so of them are doing
useful patrol work in the Docksâ
I believe it would be better if
they were sworn in as special
Constables (without pay), as they
have no status now to enable them
to arrest any one. I understand some application has
already been made about this, and I
wish to support it. Sincerely John Bay
